Clear the whole cache\\n249: \\tif req.Forced {\\n250: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n251: \\t} else {\\n252: \\t\\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\\n253: \\t\\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\\n254: \\t}\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\\n258: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n259: \\tif !req.Full {\\n260: \\t\\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\\n261: \\t\\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\\n262: \\t\\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\\n263: \\t\\treturn\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\t// Reset the status during the push.\\n266: \\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\\n267: \\tif oldPushContext != nil {\\n268: \\t\\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\\n269: \\t\\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\\n270: \\t\\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\\n271: \\t}\\n272: \\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\\n273: \\t// saved.\\n274: \\tt0 := time.Now()\\n275: \\tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\\n276: \\tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\\n277: \\tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\\n278: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"InitContext %v for push took %s\\\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\\n279: \\tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\\n280: \\n281: \\treq.Push = push\\n282: \\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\\n283: }\\n284: \\n285: func nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\\n286: \\treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\\n287: }\\n288: \\n289: // Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\\n290: // PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\\n291: // only be used for \\\"global\\\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\\n292: func (s *DiscoveryServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\\n293: \\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\\n294: }\\n295: \\n296: var fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\\\"fullpush\\\", \\\"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\\\")\\n297: \\n298: // ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n300: \\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n301: \\t\\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\\n302: \\t\\t\\tpanic(\\\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\\\")\\n303: \\t\\t}\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\\n306: \\t\\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\\n340: \\tvar req *model.PushRequest\\n341: \\n342: \\tfree := true\\n343: \\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\\n344: \\n345: \\tpush := func(req \\t\\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\\n349: \\t\\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\\n350: \\t}\\n351: \\n352: \\tpushWorker := func() {\\n353: \\t\\teventDelay := \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\tfree = false\\n369: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n42: )\\n43: \\n44: var periodicRefreshMetrics = 10 * time.Second\\n45: \\n46: type DebounceOptions struct {\\n47: \\t// DebounceAfter is the delay added to events to wait\\n48: \\t// after a registry/config event for debouncing.\\n49: \\t// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus\\n50: \\t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\\n51: \\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\\n52: \\t// delaying until things settle.\\n53: \\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\\n54: \\n55: \\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\\n56: \\t// while debouncing. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\\n83: \\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\\n84: \\n85: \\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\\n86: \\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\\n87: \\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\\n88: \\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\\n89: \\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\\n90: \\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\\n91: \\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\\n92: \\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\\n93: \\n94: \\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\\n95: \\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\\n96: \\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\\n97: \\n98: \\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\\n99: \\tpushQueue *PushQueue\\n100: \\n101: \\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\\n102: \\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\\n103: \\n104: \\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\\n105: \\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\\n106: \\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\\n107: \\n108: \\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. \\n137: \\tkrtDebugger *krt.DebugHandler\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: // NewDiscoveryServer creates DiscoveryServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures\\n141: func NewDiscoveryServer(env *model.Environment, clusterAliases map[string]string, debugger *krt.DebugHandler) *DiscoveryServer {\\n142: \\tout := &DiscoveryServer{\\n143: \\t\\tEnv: \\t\\tout.ClusterAliases[cluster.ID(alias)] = cluster.ID(clusterAliases[alias])\\n167: \\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\tout.initJwksResolver()\\n170: \\n171: \\treturn out\\n172: }\\n173: \\n174: // \\t}\\n179: \\ts.JwtKeyResolver = model.NewJwksResolver(\\n180: \\t\\tmodel.JwtPubKeyEvictionDuration, model.JwtPubKeyRefreshInterval,\\n181: \\t\\tmodel.JwtPubKeyRefreshIntervalOnFailure, s)\\n200: }\\n201: \\n202: var processStartTime = time.Now()\\n203: \\n204: // CachesSynced is called when caches have been synced so that server can accept connections.\\n205: func (s }\\n221: \\n222: // Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\\n223: func (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\\n224: \\tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\\n225: \\tdefer ticker.Stop()\\n226: \\tfor {\\n227: \\t\\tselect {\\n228: \\t\\tcase <-ticker.C:\\n229: \\t\\t\\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\\n230: \\t\\t\\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\\n231: \\t\\t\\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\\n234: \\t\\t\\t\\tout, _ response to a push request\\n247: func (s *DiscoveryServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n248: \\t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\\n249: \\tif req.Forced {\\n250: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n251: \\t} else {\\n252: \\t\\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\\n253: \\t\\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\\n254: \\t}\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\\n258: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n259: \\tif !req.Full {\\n260: \\t\\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\\n261: \\t\\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\\n262: \\t\\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\\n263: \\t\\treturn\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\t// Reset the status during the push.\\n266: \\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\\n267: \\tif oldPushContext != nil {\\n268: \\t\\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\\n269: \\t\\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\\n270: \\t\\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\\n271: \\t}\\n272: \\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\\n340: \\tvar req *model.PushRequest\\n341: \\n342: \\tfree := true\\n343: \\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\\n344: \\n345: \\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\\n346: \\t\\tpushFn(req)\\n347: \\t\\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\\n348: \\t\\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\\n349: \\t\\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\\n350: \\t}\\n351: \\n352: \\tpushWorker := func() {\\n353: \\t\\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\\n354: \\t\\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\\n355: \\t\\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\\n356: \\t\\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\\n357: \\t\\t\\tif req != nil {\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter++\\n359: \\t\\t\\t\\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\\\",\\n361: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\\n362: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\\\",\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\tfree = false\\n369: \\t\\t\\t\\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\treq = nil\\n371: \\t\\t\\t\\tdebouncedEvents = 0\\n372: \\t\\t\\t}\\n373: \\t\\t} else {\\n374: \\t\\t\\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\\n375: \\t\\t}\\n376: \\t}\\n377: \\n378: \\tfor {\\n379: \\t\\tselect {\\n380: \\t\\tcase <-freeCh:\\n381: \\t\\t\\tfree = true\\n382: \\t\\t\\tpushWorker()\\n383: \\t\\tcase r := <-ch:\\n384: \\t\\t\\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\\n385: \\t\\t\\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\\n386: \\t\\t\\t\\tr.Reason = {\\n416: \\tconfigs := \\\"\\\"\\n417: \\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\\n418: \\t\\tconfigs += key.String()\\n419: \\t\\tbreak\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\\n422: \\n457: func doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\\n458: \\tfor {\\n459: \\t\\tselect {\\n460: \\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n461: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n462: This method can be safely called concurrently,\\n531: // but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\\n532: // This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\\n533: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {\\n534: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n535: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n536: \\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\\n537: \\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\\n538: \\t\\tselect {\\n539: \\t\\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\\n540: \\t\\tdefault:\\n541: \\t\\t\\t// Initialization not complete, skip\\n542: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n543: \\t\\t}\\n544: \\t\\tclients = append(clients, con)\\n545: \\t}\\n546: \\treturn clients\\n547: }\\n548: \\n549: // SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\\n550: // Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\\n551: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\\n552: \\tclients := s.Clients()\\n553: \\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\\n554: \\t\\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\\n555: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n556: \\t\\t}\\n557: \\t\\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\\n558: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n559: \\t\\t}\\n560: \\t\\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\\n561: \\t})\\n562: \\treturn clients\\n563: }\\n564: \\n565: // AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\\n566: // Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\\n567: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\\n568: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n569: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n570: \\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\\n571: }\\n572: \\n573: func (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\\n574: \\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\\n575: \\t\\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\\n576: \\t\\treturn nil\\n577: \\t}\\n578: \\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. time.Second\n45: \n46: type DebounceOptions struct {\n47: \t// DebounceAfter is the delay added to events to wait\n48: \t// after a registry/config event for debouncing.\n49: \t// This will delay the push by at least this interval, plus\n50: \t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\n51: \t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n52: \t// delaying until things settle.\n53: \tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n54: \n55: \t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n56: \t// while debouncing. Defaults to 10 seconds. If events keep\n57: \t// showing up with no break for this time, we'll trigger a push.\n58: \tdebounceMax time.Duration\n59: \n60: \t// enableEDSDebounce indicates whether EDS pushes should be debounced.\n61: \tenableEDSDebounce bool\n62: }\n63: \n64: // DiscoveryServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs\n65: type DiscoveryServer struct {\n66: \t// Env is the model environment.\n67: \tEnv *model.Environment\n68: \n69: \t// Generators allow customizing the generated config, based on the client metadata.\n70: \t// Key is the generator type - will match the Generator metadata to set the per-connection\n71: \t// default generator, or the combination of Generator metadata and TypeUrl to select a\n72: \t// different generator for a type.\n73: \t// Normal istio clients use the default generator - will not be impacted by this.\n74: \tGenerators map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator\n75: \n76: \t// ProxyNeedsPush is a function that determines whether a push can be completely skipped. Individual generators\n77: \t// may also choose to not send any updates.\n78: \tProxyNeedsPush func(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest) (*model.PushRequest, bool)\n79: \n80: \t// concurrentPushLimit is a semaphore that limits the amount of concurrent XDS pushes.\n81: \tconcurrentPushLimit chan struct{}\n82: \t// RequestRateLimit limits the number of new XDS requests allowed. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n83: \tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n84: \n85: \t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n86: \tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n87: \t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n88: \t// received, process, and stored in the push context. \tkrtDebugger *krt.DebugHandler\n138: }\n139: \n140: // NewDiscoveryServer creates DiscoveryServer that sources data from Pilot's internal mesh data structures\n141: func NewDiscoveryServer(env // Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\n223: func (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n224: \tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\n225: plus\n\t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\n\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n\t// delaying until things settle.\n\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n\n\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n\t// while debouncing. This helps prevent thundering hurd of incoming requests.\n\tRequestRateLimit *rate.Limiter\n\n\t// InboundUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has received\n\tInboundUpdates *atomic.Int64\n\t// CommittedUpdates describes the number of configuration updates the discovery server has\n\t// received, process, and stored in the push context. If this number is less than InboundUpdates,\n\t// there are updates we have not yet processed.\n\t// Note: This does not mean that all proxies have received these configurations; it is strictly\n\t// the push context, which means that the next push to a proxy will receive this configuration.\n\tCommittedUpdates *atomic.Int64\n\n\t// pushChannel is the buffer used for debouncing.\n\t// after debouncing the pushRequest will be sent to pushQueue\n\tpushChannel chan *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// pushQueue is the buffer that used after debounce and before the real xds push.\n\tpushQueue *PushQueue\n\n\t// debugHandlers is the list of all the supported debug handlers.\n\tdebugHandlers map[string]string\n\n\t// adsClients reflect active gRPC channels, for both ADS and EDS.\n\tadsClients      map[string]*Connection\n\tadsClientsMutex sync.RWMutex\n\n\t// Authenticators for XDS requests. {\n\t\ts.JwtKeyResolver.Close()\n\t}\n}\n\n// Register adds the ADS handler to the grpc server\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Register(rpcs *grpc.Server) {\n\t// Register v3 server\n\tdiscovery.RegisterAggregatedDiscoveryServiceServer(rpcs, s)\n}\n\nvar processStartTime = time.Now()\n\n// CachesSynced is called when caches have been synced so that server can accept connections.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) CachesSynced() {\n\tlog.Infof(\"All caches have been synced up in %v, marking server ready\", time.Since(s.DiscoveryStartTime))\n\ts.serverReady.Store(true)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) IsServerReady() bool {\n\treturn s.serverReady.Load()\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Start(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tgo s.WorkloadEntryController.Run(stopCh)\n\tgo s.handleUpdates(stopCh)\n\tgo s.periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh)\n\tgo s.sendPushes(stopCh)\n\tgo s.Cache.Run(stopCh)\n}\n\n// Push metrics are updated periodically (10s default)\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) periodicRefreshMetrics(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(periodicRefreshMetrics)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\n\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n\t\t\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\n\t\t\t\tmodel.LastPushStatus = push\n\t\t\t\tpush.UpdateMetrics()\n\t\t\t\tout, _ := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n\t\t\t\tif string(out) != \"{}\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push Status: %s\", string(out))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// dropCacheForRequest clears the cache in response to a push request\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) dropCacheForRequest(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// If we don't know what updated, cannot safely cache. Clear the whole cache\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Otherwise, just clear the updated configs\n\t\ts.Cache.Clear(req.ConfigsUpdated)\n\t}\n}\n\n// Push is called to push changes on config updates using ADS.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Push(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\treq.Push = s.globalPushContext()\n\t\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n\t\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\t// Reset the status during the push.\n\toldPushContext := s.globalPushContext()\n\tif oldPushContext != nil {\n\t\toldPushContext.OnConfigChange()\n\t\t// Push the previous push Envoy metrics.\n\t\tenvoyfilter.RecordMetrics()\n\t}\n\t// PushContext is reset after a config change. Previous status is\n\t// saved.\n\tt0 := time.Now()\n\tversionLocal := s.NextVersion()\n\tpush := s.initPushContext(req, oldPushContext, versionLocal)\n\tinitContextTime := time.Since(t0)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"InitContext %v for push took %s\", versionLocal, initContextTime)\n\tpushContextInitTime.Record(initContextTime.Seconds())\n\n\treq.Push = push\n\ts.AdsPushAll(req)\n}\n\nfunc nonce(noncePrefix string) string {\n\treturn noncePrefix + uuid.New().String()\n}\n\n// Returns the global push context. This should be used with caution; generally the proxy-specific\n// PushContext should be used to get the current state in the context of a single proxy. This should\n// only be used for \"global\" lookups, such as initiating a new push to all proxies.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) globalPushContext() *model.PushContext {\n\treturn s.Env.PushContext()\n}\n\nvar fullPushLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"fullpush\", \"logs details about why Istio is triggering a full push\")\n\n// ConfigUpdate implements ConfigUpdater interface, used to request pushes.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigUpdate(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Service) {\n\t\t\tpanic(\"assertion failed kind.Service can not be set in ConfigKey\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address) {\n\t\t// This is a bit like clearing EDS cache on EndpointShard update. Because Address\n\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n\t\t// the cache.\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t}\n\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n\t}\n\ts.pushChannel <- req\n}\n\n// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n}\n\n// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\nfunc debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n\n\tpushCounter := 0\n\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n\n\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n\n\tfree := true\n\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n\n\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tpushWorker := func() {\n\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n\t\t\tif req != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfree = false\n\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n\t\t\t\treq = nil\n\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-freeCh:\n\t\t\tfree = true\n\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\tcase r := <-ch:\n\t\t\t// If reason is not set, record it as an unknown reason\n\t\t\tif len(r.Reason) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tr.Reason = model.NewReasonStats(model.UnknownTrigger)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !opts.enableEDSDebounce && !r.Full {\n\t\t\t\t// trigger push now, just for EDS\n\t\t\t\tgo func(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t\t\t\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\t\t\t\tupdateSent.Inc()\n\t\t\t\t}(r)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastConfigUpdateTime = time.Now()\n\t\t\tif debouncedEvents == 0 {\n\t\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter)\n\t\t\t\tstartDebounce = lastConfigUpdateTime\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdebouncedEvents++\n\n\t\t\treq = req.Merge(r)\n\t\tcase <-timeChan:\n\t\t\tif free {\n\t\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc configsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n\tconfigs := \"\"\n\tfor key := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tconfigs += key.String()\n\t\tbreak\n\t}\n\tif len(req.ConfigsUpdated) > 1 {\n\t\tmore := \" and \" + strconv.Itoa(len(req.ConfigsUpdated)-1) + \" more configs\"\n\t\tconfigs += more\n\t}\n\treturn configs\n}\n\nfunc reasonsUpdated(req *model.PushRequest) string {\n\tvar (\n\t\treason0, reason1            model.TriggerReason\n\t\treason0Cnt, reason1Cnt, idx int\n\t)\n\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n\t\tif idx == 0 {\n\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tidx++\n\t}\n\n\tswitch len(req.Reason) {\n\tcase 0:\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\tcase 1:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt)\n\tcase 2:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// We can send to it until it is full, then it will block until a pushes finishes and reads from it.\n\t\t\t// This limits the number of pushes that can happen concurrently\n\t\t\tsemaphore <- struct{}{}\n\n\t\t\t// Get the next proxy to push. Note: while this\n// method is technically thread safe (there are no data races), it should not be called in parallel;\n// if it is, then we may start two push context creations (say A, and B), but then write them in\n// reverse order, leaving us with a final version of A, which may be incomplete.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initPushContext(req *model.PushRequest, oldPushContext *model.PushContext, version string) *model.PushContext {\n\tpush := model.NewPushContext()\n\tpush.PushVersion = version\n\tpush.JwtKeyResolver = s.JwtKeyResolver\n\tpush.InitContext(s.Env, oldPushContext, req)\n\n\ts.dropCacheForRequest(req)\n\ts.Env.SetPushContext(push)\n\n\treturn push\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) sendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdoSendPushes(stopCh, s.concurrentPushLimit, s.pushQueue)\n}\n\n// Shutdown shuts down DiscoveryServer components.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Shutdown() {\n\ts.closeJwksResolver()\n\ts.pushQueue.ShutDown()\n}\n\n// Clients returns all currently connected clients. This method can be safely called concurrently,\n// but care should be taken with the underlying objects (ie model.Proxy) to ensure proper locking.\n// This method returns only fully initialized connections; for all connections, use AllClients\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Clients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\tclients := make([]*Connection, 0, len(s.adsClients))\n\tfor _, con := range s.adsClients {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Initialization not complete, skip\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tclients = append(clients, con)\n\t}\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// SortedClients returns all currently connected clients in an ordered manner.\n// Sorting order priority is as follows: ClusterID, Namespace, ID.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) SortedClients() []*Connection {\n\tclients := s.Clients()\n\tsort.Slice(clients, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetClusterID().String() < clients[j].proxy.GetClusterID().String() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\tif clients[i].proxy.GetNamespace() < clients[j].proxy.GetNamespace() {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn clients[i].proxy.GetID() < clients[j].proxy.GetID()\n\t})\n\treturn clients\n}\n\n// AllClients returns all connected clients, per Clients, but additionally includes uninitialized connections\n// Warning: callers must take care not to rely on the con.proxy field being set\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) AllClients() []*Connection {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn maps.Values(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) WaitForRequestLimit(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tif s.RequestRateLimit.Limit() == 0 {\n\t\t// Allow opt out when rate limiting is set to 0qps\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\t// Give a bit of time for queue to clear out, but if not fail fast. Client will connect to another\n\t// instance in best case, or retry with backoff.\n\twait, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\treturn s.RequestRateLimit.Wait(wait)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) NextVersion() string {\n\treturn time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339) + \"/\" + strconv.FormatUint(s.pushVersion.Inc(), 10)\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":61,"oldLines":8,"newStart":61,"newLines":8,"lines":["   enableEDSDebounce bool"," }"," ","-// DiscoveryServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs","-type DiscoveryServer struct {","+// XDSServer is Pilot's gRPC implementation for Envoy's xds APIs","+type XDSServer struct {","   // Env is the model environment.","   Env *model.Environment"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tpush := s.globalPushContext()\n\t\t\tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n\t\t\tif model.LastPushStatus != push {\n\t\t\t\tmodel.LastPushStatus = Because Address\n\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n\t\t// the cache.\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t}\n\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n\t}\n\ts.pushChannel <- req\n}\n\n// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n}\n\n// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\nfunc debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n\n\tpushCounter := 0\n\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n\n\t// Keeps track of the push requests. If updates are debounce they will be merged.\n\tvar req *model.PushRequest\n\n\tfree := true\n\tfreeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)\n\n\tpush := func(req *model.PushRequest, debouncedEvents int, startDebounce time.Time) {\n\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tpushWorker := func() {\n\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n\t\t\tif req != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, reasonsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for config %s: %v since last change, %v since last push, full=%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tpushCounter, debouncedEvents, configsUpdated(req),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tquietTime, eventDelay, req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfree = false\n\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n\t\t\t\treq = nil\n\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeChan = time.After(opts.DebounceAfter - quietTime)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-freeCh:\n\t\t\tfree = true\n\t\t\tpushWorker()\n\t\tcase r := <-ch:\n\t\t\t// If reason is not set, record plus\n\t// the time getting subsequent events. If no change is\n\t// detected the push will happen, otherwise we'll keep\n\t// delaying until things settle.\n\tDebounceAfter time.Duration\n\n\t// debounceMax is the maximum time to wait for events\n\t// while debouncing. Because Address\n\t\t// types are fetched dynamically, they are not part of the same protections, so we need to clear\n\t\t// the cache.\n\t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n\t}\n\tinboundConfigUpdates.Increment()\n\ts.InboundUpdates.Inc()\n\tif req.Full && fullPushLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tconfigs := slices.Sort(slices.Map(req.ConfigsUpdated.UnsortedList(), model.ConfigKey.String))\n\t\treasons := maps.Keys(req.Reason)\n\t\tfullPushLog.Debugf(\"full push triggered configs=%v reasons=%v\", configs, reasons)\n\t}\n\ts.pushChannel <- req\n}\n\n// Debouncing and push request happens in a separate thread, it uses locks\n// and we want to avoid complications, ConfigUpdate may already hold other locks.\n// handleUpdates processes events from pushChannel\n// It ensures that at minimum minQuiet time has elapsed since the last event before processing it.\n// It also ensures that at most maxDelay is elapsed between receiving an event and processing it.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleUpdates(stopCh <-chan struct{}) {\n\tdebounce(s.pushChannel, stopCh, s.DebounceOptions, s.Push, s.CommittedUpdates)\n}\n\n// The debounce helper function is implemented to enable mocking\nfunc debounce(ch chan *model.PushRequest, stopCh <-chan struct{}, opts DebounceOptions, pushFn func(req *model.PushRequest), updateSent *atomic.Int64) {\n\tvar timeChan <-chan time.Time\n\tvar startDebounce time.Time\n\tvar lastConfigUpdateTime time.Time\n\n\tpushCounter := 0\n\tdebouncedEvents := 0\n\n\t// Keeps track of the push requests. startDebounce time.Time) {\n\t\tpushFn(req)\n\t\tupdateSent.Add(int64(debouncedEvents))\n\t\tdebounceTime.Record(time.Since(startDebounce).Seconds())\n\t\tfreeCh <- struct{}{}\n\t}\n\n\tpushWorker := func() {\n\t\teventDelay := time.Since(startDebounce)\n\t\tquietTime := time.Since(lastConfigUpdateTime)\n\t\t// it has been too long or quiet enough\n\t\tif eventDelay >= opts.debounceMax || quietTime >= opts.DebounceAfter {\n\t\t\tif req != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpushCounter++\n\t\t\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Push debounce stable[%d] %d for reason %s: %v since last req.Full)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfree = false\n\t\t\t\tgo push(req, debouncedEvents, startDebounce)\n\t\t\t\treq = nil\n\t\t\t\tdebouncedEvents = 0\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ttimeChan = reason1Cnt, idx int\n\t)\n\tfor r, cnt := range req.Reason {\n\t\tif idx == 0 {\n\t\t\treason0, reason0Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else if idx == 1 {\n\t\t\treason1, reason1Cnt = r, cnt\n\t\t} else fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %s:%d\", reason0, reason0Cnt, reason1, reason1Cnt)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s:%d and %d(%d) more reasons\", reason0, reason0Cnt, len(req.Reason)-1,\n\t\t\treq.Reason.Count()-reason0Cnt)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc doSendPushes(stopCh <-chan struct{}, semaphore chan struct{}, queue *PushQueue) {\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase During\n   191→\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n   192→\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n   193→\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n   194→\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n   195→\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n   196→\t}\n   197→\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n   198→\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n   199→\t// clusters correctly. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\nvar KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t} else {\n\t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n\n\t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t} else {\n\t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n\t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n}\n\n// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then add any undeclared types\n\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n\t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n\tdone func()\n}\n\nfunc newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n\treturn &Connection{\n\t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Close() {\n\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n\tpushEv.done()\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\nvar KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t} else {\n\t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n\n\t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n}\n\nfunc (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t} else {\n\t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n\t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n}\n\n// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then add any undeclared types\n\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n\t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered,
